title: One thing I love about...Shadows of Brimstone
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2l0-duQwSfI

---

Daniel (@dungeondive) celebrates the one thing he loves most about Shadows of Brimstone: its expansive bestiary. Where so many dungeon crawlers fall short on enemy variety, Shadows of Brimstone excels — provided you invest in the expansions.

The video showcases Daniel's full enemy collection in action for the Gates of Valhalla campaign: Targa librarians, centurions, trolls, grimians, tentacles, guardians, magma giants, Shakari Nomad Crusaders, custodians, flesh stalkers, void swarms, Goliath, trune hunters, frost giants, and archive towers. Big bosses include Belial, the Burrower, Sand Kraken, Ancient One, and Sho Ryu the Dragon King.

Each enemy's large information sheet provides combat stats, elite/brutal variants for scaling difficulty, and unique abilities that force different tactical approaches. The genre-busting nature of the game — weird west, horror, sci-fi, Asian mythology, Viking mythology — means your threat deck can mix robots with demons, tribal warriors with void aliens.

Daniel demonstrates how easy it is to create themed subsets: an undead deck, a robot deck, an Asian mythology deck. The poker-sized threat cards shuffle into custom threat decks, and mixing enemy types creates emergent tactical variety as creatures with ranged attacks, melee specialisations, magic powers, and area effects interact on the same battlefield.
